A drug-free psychedelic experience.

Not just music. A new way of listening to yourself.

Acoustic States is a science-backed platform for sonic journeys designed to help you shift emotional states, explore new perspectives, and discover different ways of relating to your life.

Join the WaitlistHow it works
Scroll down⌄

For those feeling burnt out, disconnected, emotionally blocked, or ready for something to move.

Sound can move you, if you let it in.

Acoustic States is powered by three principles: attention, intention, and imagination.

Drawing on research in neural entrainment, music reward, nervous system regulation, the entropic brain, and ritual practices, we guide you into a space where you stop simply listening to sound and start moving inside it. We call this ensounded cognition.

The result is not just brain stimulation.
It is absorption.
In deep states of absorption, sound can shift emotion, loosen rigid patterns, and open new perspectives.
The experience opens possibilities.
What you do with them is the change. That’s why Integration is key.

Not just binaural beats or music.

Not meditation.

A method for entering acoustic states of consciousness.

Music is a natural narcotic.

You’ve felt it.

Neuroscience confirms it.

A song that gave you chills→Dopamine + endogenous opioid release
A melody that brought back a forgotten memory→Hippocampal memory network activation
A voice that made you feel safe→Oxytocin + vagal regulation
A chord that moved you to tears→Endogenous opioid release
A rhythm that changed your mood in seconds→Dopamine + noradrenaline release

But the effect can go beyond mood.

When we listen deeply, music doesn’t just change how we feel.It can change how we perceive our reality.

Our approach draws on peer-reviewed research in music neuroscience.
How it works

Every journey is unique to you.

Adapting to where you are physically and emotionally.

Connect wearable optional
Apple Watch, Oura, Whoop, or Garmin for real-time HRV feedback during your journey.
Choose your setting
Listen alone, join a group session.
Target Zone
Body
Ground, soften, release.

Mind
Clear, focus, reframe.

Imagination
Go deeper into images, symbols, memories, and new associations.
Choose your dose
Then choose your dose: Micro for a gentle reset, Macro for deeper reflection, or Hero for a full immersive journey.
Listen, come back, reflect and integrate
Once your sonic medicine is ready, you just have to close your eyes and listen.

Afterwards, you can reflect on what arose and gently bring the experience into your life.
key features
Discover what works for you
Acoustic States connects the dots across your sessions, helping you understand your patterns with more perspective. You can revisit past sessions, explore reflections, observe changes across multiple sessions, and download your session report or share it with your therapist, coach, or mental health practitioner.
Track your heart
A healthy heart does not beat like a machine. It adapts moment by moment to breath, emotion, safety and the world around you. HRV is one indicator of resilience: your body’s ability to adapt, recover, and return to balance. Music influences breathing patterns, shifting the rhythm of the heart and the state of the nervous system.
Keep the memory
After each journey, you can upload your own image or generate one from your reflection. Each visual memory is linked to the session, the intention, and the artist who shaped the journey. Because healing doesn’t have to be boring and too serious.
Journey data
State: ReleaseDose: MacroHRV trend: responsiveReflection: vivid imagery
Personal patterns across sessions

No AI-generated music.

Acoustic States is built with musicians, composers, and sound artists who treat music as more than entertainment. They work with ritual, emotion, spirituality, and the transformative power of sound.

Every artist is visible, credited, and fairly paid for the worlds they help create.

Healing often begins when
the story changes.

Psychedelic-assisted therapy has shown that altered states can create powerful conditions for change. The effect may not come only from the substance itself, but from the temporary window it opens: a state where perception becomes more flexible, emotions can move differently, and old meanings may begin to reorganise.

Acoustic States uses carefully designed sound to guide people into intentional listening states that support reflection, emotional processing, and perspective change, not as a replacement for therapy, but as an additional tool for people seeking new ways to process, reconnect, and grow.

Curious to try Acoustic States?
Book a private session.

1:1 Session

Not dissociation,but new associations!

Made by someone who needed another way in

Acoustic States was born from my PhD research in neuroaesthetics, immersive sound, and consciousness studies, but also from a lifetime of using music as a way to survive, understand, and transform experience.

After being diagnosed with C-PTSD, I became even more aware of how limited, and sometimes difficult, words can be when we are trying to process pain, memory, or change.

Psychedelic therapy transformed my life, but music gave me something equally important: perspective, continuity, and a sustainable practice for growth. It helped me make sense of experiences that language alone could never have reached.

Through music, both as a performer and as a listener, I found a way to reconnect with myself, not through dissociation, but through new association.

Acoustic States is my attempt to make transformative sonic experiences more accessible: without drugs, dogma, wellness clichés, or spiritual performance.

To learn more, share feedback, or start a conversation, you can get in touch.

Get in touch

Physical immersive installations

Alongside the app, we also offer in-person group sessions. While individual listening can become a powerful practice at home, collective experiences bring a different kind of depth: the sense of being held, witnessed, and moved alongside others.

Acoustic States is building a new approach to mental wellbeing through sound, measurable, intentional, and grounded in research.

Frequent questions and answers

What exactly is Acoustic States?

Acoustic States is a science backed protocol to induce altered states of consciousness just with sound.

It is not a music streaming service, not a meditation app, and not white noise.

It is a new category: intentional listening for state change.

Is this a medical or clinical product?

No.

Acoustic States is currently a wellness and listening platform. It is not a medical device, therapy, or treatment.

We take wellbeing seriously and are building with scientific and ethical care, but we do not make clinical claims.

Therapists, coaches, and integration practitioners may be available as optional external support, but Acoustic States itself does not provide therapy or medical care.

Is there research behind this?

Yes. Acoustic States is built on research in psychoacoustics, music neuroscience, neuroaesthetics, and nervous system regulation. We're also running our own trials to measure how sound experiences shift emotional states, embodiment, and wellbeing. Early feedback has been promising.

Still have questions?

Visit our FAQ to learn more about the platform, the science, and the listening experience.

What should I expect during a journey?

Every journey is different, but you can expect guided vocal and instrumental sound, silence, and time for your own inner process. Some people see vivid imagery. Others feel profound bodily sensations—relaxation, tingling, warmth, movement. Some sit quietly with their own thoughts. There's no "right" experience. The guidance is there to support you, not to direct you toward a specific outcome.

Do I need a wearable?

No. You'll get full access to guided journeys, adaptive agentic guidance, and integration support without one. A compatible wearable (Apple Watch, Oura Ring, Whoop Band, or Garmin) lets you see real-time heart rate variability feedback during the experience and helps the system adapt more precisely to your state. But it's optional.

How do I connect my wearable?

During setup, you'll be prompted to authorize access to your wearable's data. We integrate with Apple HealthKit (Apple Watch), Oura, Whoop, and Garmin Connect. You grant permission through your wearable's app or account settings—Acoustic States never stores your raw HRV data directly. It's used during your session to adapt the guidance and then securely deleted.

What's the difference between Micro, Macro, and Hero journeys?

Journeys vary on time, intensity and characteristics of the sound.

Micro - 15 -20 minutes. Perfect for a quick state shift: clarity, calm, or reset. Great for busy days or testing if the platform works for you.

Macro - 36-50 minutes. A deeper journey for emotional movement, self-reflection, and nervous system recalibration. Most people find this the "sweet spot."

Hero - 60+ minutes. A full ritual experience designed for deeper transformation, expanded awareness, and significant integration work. Ideal when you have time and intention to go deep.

Can I do a group journey with friends?

Yes. You can join scheduled group sessions or arrange a private group journey with friends. Group sessions use ambient HRV feedback (not individual data) and shared guidance. It's a different experience from solo—you feel the collective energy while maintaining your own inner space.

How long does integration take?

There's no timeline. Integration happens at your pace. After your journey, you'll have structured prompts, reflection questions, and suggestions for bringing the experience into your daily life. Some people integrate in hours. Others sit with it for weeks. Acoustic States gives you tools and time.

Can I share my session report with my therapist?

Yes. After each journey, you can download a session report that includes your reflection, your intentions, the sonic arc you experienced, and HRV data if you were wearing a wearable. You can share this with your therapist, coach, or mental health practitioner as material for your ongoing work together. Many practitioners find this context useful.

What if I don't like my first journey?

Try another. Your first experience gives the system information about what works for you, so the next journey will be more personalized. But if Acoustic States just isn't resonating after a few attempts, we want to know. Reach out—we're building this with real user feedback.

What privacy protections do you have?

Your session data (reflections, intentions, HRV data if connected) is encrypted and stored securely. We don't sell your data. Wearable data is used during your session and then deleted—we don't store it long-term. You can download your data anytime or request deletion. Full details are in our Privacy Policy.

Can I go back and listen to an old journey again?

Yes. All your past journeys are saved. You can revisit the sonic experience, re-read your reflections, or download the session report. But each new journey will be fresh—agentic and adapted to your current state.

What if I'm skeptical this will work?

Good instinct. Don't take anyone's word for it. Try a Micro journey. Some people feel profound shifts immediately. Others feel subtler changes over time. Skepticism is healthy—what matters is your direct experience. We're not asking for faith, just 15 minutes of listening.

Do you use AI-generated music?

No. Every sonic journey is composed and recorded by real musicians, sound artists, and composers. We work directly with artists who care about consciousness, emotion, and the transformative power of sound. Artists are credited and ethically paid for their work. No AI-generated content.

What happens if the experience feels overwhelming?

You can pause anytime. The guidance includes grounding techniques if you feel activated or uncomfortable. After the session, integration is designed to help process intensity. If something comes up that feels big, that's material for a therapist or coach, Acoustic States is a tool, not a replacement for professional support.

How is this different from using Spotify with binaural beats?

Spotify is passive music streaming. Acoustic States combines real-time adaptive guidance, specially composed sonic journeys, nervous system biofeedback, and integration support. The guidance adapts to your state as you listen. The structure moves you through a deliberate arc. And you have tools to make sense of what happened afterward. It's a protocol, not just a playlist.

How often should I do journeys?

Based on neuroscience research on neuroplasticity and consciousness work, here's what we recommend:

Micro journeys (10-15 min): At least 5 times per week. Research on music-based interventions shows that 3 times weekly begins to trigger measurable neuroplasticity in motor and auditory cortexes. More frequent engagement accelerates and sustains neural adaptation. Micro journeys are designed for this—they fit into daily life and create consistent nervous system regulation.

Macro journeys (30-45 min): At least 1 per week. Studies at the University of Toronto found that daily listening to personally meaningful music over three weeks produced measurable changes in brain connectivity and white matter, correlating with improved memory performance. One deeper weekly journey moves emotional material and allows for more substantial integration work.

Hero journeys (60+ min): Once per month, or as needed. Hero sessions are for threshold experiences—when you're ready for transformation, processing major life shifts, or going deep. These work best spaced out with integration time between them.

Is this safe if I have a medical condition?

Acoustic States is a wellness platform, not medical treatment. However, sound experiences can affect nervous system state and consciousness, so we have important safety guidelines.

Please consult your healthcare provider before starting if you have:

  • Active or current psychotic episodes
  • History of untreated psychosis or dissociation
  • Heart conditions (pacemaker, arrhythmia)
  • Epilepsy triggered by sound or light
  • Severe or unmanaged anxiety
  • Recent trauma without professional support

Why these exclusions matter: sound structured in rhythmic forms can trigger altered states of consciousness and affect neural activity and subjective experience significantly. For people with untreated dissociation or psychotic disorders, this can destabilize rather than support. We want Acoustic States to help you feel more grounded and present—not further disconnected from reality.

If you're in treatment or recovery: you may be able to use Acoustic States with your therapist or psychiatrist's support. Many practitioners find that integrated sound work helps their clients—but it needs professional oversight in your case. Reach out at anaivivcaro@gmail.com and let us know your situation. We can discuss whether Acoustic States is right for you and how to use it safely alongside your care.

If you're unsure: ask your healthcare provider. If they say no, that's the answer. If they say yes with conditions (like working with a therapist), we're happy to collaborate.

We're not gatekeeping to be restrictive. We're gatekeeping because this matters.

Can I read some papers about this?
  1. Aparicio-Terrés, A., Benítez-García, M., & Guevara, M. A. (2025). Rhythms of relief: perspectives on neurocognitive mechanisms of music interventions in ADHD. Frontiers in Psychology, 16, 1476928.
  2. Fischer, C., Heymann, E., Tsouli, S., Thaut, M. H., & Ganguli, M. (2021). On Repeat: Listening to favorite music improves brain plasticity, cognitive performance of Alzheimer's patients. Journal of Alzheimer's Disease, 88(4), 1623-1635.
  3. Advanced Brain Technologies. (2025). Neuroplasticity and The Listening Program: Rewiring the Brain for Optimal Performance.
  4. Reybrouck, M., Vuust, P., & Brattico, E. (2021). Neural correlates of music listening: Does the music matter? Brain Sciences, 11(12), 1553.
  5. Luke, D. P. (2013). Psychedelic sound and perception.
  6. Center for Deep Listening. (2024). Deep Listening: The Pauline Oliveros practice.
Privacy

Privacy

Your listening is yours. Your data is yours. Your inner world stays yours.

Acoustic States is built for intimate listening, emotional reflection, and personal transformation.

That means privacy is not an extra feature. It is part of the foundation.

Personalisation Is Always Your Choice

You decide how much you want Acoustic States to know.

Share only what feels useful: your listening preferences, emotional check-ins, reflections, or wearable data.

Your Inner World Is Not a Product

We do not sell or rent your personal data.

We do not use your reflections to advertise to you. Never EVER.

When you choose to share data, Acoustic States can learn from your experience without making you personally identifiable. The information you choose to share helps the recommendation engine understand which sonic journeys support you best.

We use privacy-conscious methods to understand broader patterns: which sounds, doses, and listening states tend to support different needs.

This helps improve the recommendation engine now, and will support future research into sound-based wellbeing.

Protected by Design

We follow privacy-first principles from the start: data minimisation, transparency, security, and user control. Acoustic States is designed to align with UK GDPR and relevant US privacy standards, including California privacy rights.

Contact Us

Have a question, feedback, or want to collaborate? We'd love to hear from you.
